name: Community & Teachers
description_short: Not-for-profit member-owned credit union serving Rhode Island residents with savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and financial education since 1960.
description_long:
Community & Teachers Federal Credit Union was founded in 1960 by eight teachers as a cooperative financial institution dedicated to serving members in Rhode Island. Originally restricted to school department employees, the credit union expanded under a community charter to welcome anyone who lives, works, worships, or attends school in select Rhode Island counties, as well as their family members. This evolution reflects the organization's commitment to broadening financial access beyond its founding teacher base.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including share savings accounts, share certificates, personal and business loans, mortgages, and a virtual branch for online banking. Members can access financial calculators, order checks, purchase insurance products, and utilize the mortgage center for home financing needs. The organization provides both in-person and drive-up teller services, shared branching access, and electronic banking capabilities including ATM access, online account management, bill payment, check deposits, and fund transfers.

Community & Teachers distinguishes itself through personalized member education, offering one-on-one financial consultations to help members understand credit reports and navigate major financial decisions like home purchases. The credit union actively invests in community development, supporting charities, local events, youth and sports programs, and educational advancement for both teens and adults. As a not-for-profit cooperative structure (NCUA-insured), the organization prioritizes member benefits over shareholder returns.

The credit union operates with limited hours (Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-6pm, Saturday 9am-12pm), and some services require appointments, which may inconvenience members needing flexibility. While the website demonstrates community commitment and service breadth, specific rate competitiveness, loan approval criteria, and membership eligibility details are not detailed online. The organization is well-suited for Rhode Island residents seeking a community-focused alternative to traditional banks with personalized service.
services: Share savings accounts and member deposits
Personal loans with published rates available online
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
Virtual branch online banking with bill payment and check deposit
Shared branching access at partner credit union locations
ATM access and electronic funds transfers
One-on-one financial consultations and credit report education
pros: Not-for-profit cooperative structure returns profits to members rather than shareholders
NCUA-insured member accounts providing federal protection
Over 55 years of local history and community engagement in Rhode Island
Personalized one-on-one financial consultations with representatives included
Shared branching network expands access beyond single location
Comprehensive services including mortgages, loans, savings, and insurance under one institution
Active community investment in charities, youth programs, and educational advancement
cons: Limited service hours (closes at 4pm weekdays except Friday, closed Sundays) restrict accessibility
Membership restricted to Rhode Island residents in specific counties, excluding broader geographic reach
Many in-person services require advance appointments, reducing walk-in convenience
No specific rate information published online for loans or savings products
Coin transaction services require in-lobby appointment only, creating friction for basic services
best_for: Rhode Island residents seeking personalized financial advice and community-focused banking
Homebuyers in eligible counties needing mortgage guidance and education
Current and former school employees and their families with credit union heritage preference
Community-minded savers wanting cooperative ownership over traditional bank profits
diagnosis: Community & Teachers Federal Credit Union is best for Rhode Island residents in eligible counties who value personalized service, community involvement, and cooperative ownership over profit margins. The main caveat is limited geographic eligibility (Rhode Island only) and restricted service hours requiring advance planning for appointments, making it less suitable for those requiring 24/7 access or living outside the service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
address: 1169 S Broadway, East Providence, RI 02914, USA
meta_description: Community & Teachers — NCUA-insured credit union in East Providence, RI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
